seo.title: Happy Horse 1.1 | Native Audio AI Video Model | MaxVideoAI
seo.description: Use Happy Horse 1.1 on MaxVideoAI for text-to-video, image-to-video, reference-to-video, native audio, and lip-sync.

overview: Happy Horse 1.1 is Alibaba's current AI video model on MaxVideoAI for text prompts, first-frame images, and up to nine reference images with native synchronized audio and lip-sync.
pricingNotes: Happy Horse 1.1 uses the current Fal rates for text, image, and reference-to-video runs. Video edit remains on legacy Happy Horse 1.0.

hero.title: Happy Horse 1.1 - Alibaba video generation with native audio
hero.intro: Generate from text, animate an image, or guide a scene with up to nine references from the current Happy Horse 1.1 model card.
hero.badge: Text · Image · Reference · 720p/1080p · Native audio · Lip-sync

items.title: Text, image, and reference-to-video production workflows
Creator-style ads with synchronized native audio

technicalOverview.label: Workflows
technicalOverview.body: Text-to-video, image-to-video, and reference-to-video are exposed as one current Happy Horse 1.1 model in MaxVideoAI.

technicalOverview.label: Reference images
technicalOverview.body: 1-9 images, addressed as character1 through character9 in the prompt.

technicalOverview.label: Video edit
technicalOverview.body: Not exposed on Happy Horse 1.1. Use the legacy Happy Horse 1.0 video-edit route when a source clip must be edited.

technicalOverview.label: Audio
technicalOverview.body: Native synchronized audio and lip-sync are treated as part of the generation.

promptStructure.title: Prompting Happy Horse 1.1
promptStructure.quote: Subject + action + camera + audio cue + workflow-specific references.
promptStructure.description: Keep prompts direct and assign each reference a clear role. In reference-to-video, name each image as character1, character2, and so on.
steps: Choose the workflow: text, image, or reference-to-video
Describe the subject and visible action
Specify native audio or dialogue cues
Name references explicitly when using reference-to-video

tips.title: Tips and limits
items: Use T2V for fresh ideas and spokesperson-style native audio shots.
Use I2V when a key visual or first frame is already approved.
Use reference-to-video when identity, wardrobe, product shape, or character continuity matters.
Use Happy Horse 1.0 only when the job specifically needs legacy video edit.

prompts.title: Native audio launch clip
prompts.prompt: 16:9 studio product launch clip, a confident creator speaks directly to camera with natural lip-sync, warm key light, subtle camera push, clean room tone and soft launch music.

prompts.title: Reference two-character demo
prompts.prompt: Use character1 and character2 from the reference images in a cinematic two-person product demo, consistent wardrobe, stable faces, natural synchronized dialogue.

prompts.title: Image-to-video key art
prompts.prompt: Bring the uploaded campaign still to life with a smooth dolly push, subtle fabric and hair motion, polished ad lighting, and synchronized ambience.

faqs.q: What inputs does Happy Horse 1.1 support?
faqs.a: MaxVideoAI exposes Happy Horse 1.1 as one current model with text-to-video, image-to-video, and reference-to-video workflows.
Yes. Happy Horse 1.1 is treated as a native-audio model with synchronized speech and lip-sync integrated into the generation flow.

faqs.q: Does Happy Horse 1.1 support video edit?
faqs.a: No. Video edit remains available through the legacy Happy Horse 1.0 route.

custom.galleryIntro: Review the model page clips for native audio, lip-sync, and reference-to-video behavior. Comparison pages intentionally stay text/spec focused for this launch.
quickPricingItems: Use 720p for cheaper iteration across text, image, and reference-to-video.
Use 1080p when the shot is ready for review or publication.
Use Happy Horse 1.0 only when an approved source clip needs legacy video edit.
custom.relatedSubtitle: Use Happy Horse 1.1 when native audio, lip-sync, and text/image/reference workflows matter, without placing it above Seedance, Kling, or Veo for flagship quality.

relatedItems.title: Seedance 2.0
relatedItems.description: Compare against Seedance when the decision is multimodal reference control, native audio behavior, and stronger production continuity.

relatedItems.title: Google Veo 3.1
relatedItems.description: Compare against Veo when premium cinematic realism and audio-native output are the main criteria.

custom.finalPara1: Happy Horse 1.1 fits best when the brief needs one current Alibaba model for prompt generation, image animation, reference-guided consistency, native audio, and lip-sync; choose Seedance, Kling, or Veo first when raw realism, image quality, and motion control are the priority.
custom.finalPara2: For launch work, start with text or reference-to-video to establish the shot, then use Happy Horse 1.0 only if an approved source clip needs a directed legacy edit.

hero.title: Happy Horse 1.1
hero.subtitle: Native audio, lip-sync, image-to-video and reference-to-video in one current Alibaba route.
hero.paragraph: Use Happy Horse 1.1 when a shot needs synchronized speech or sound from text, a starting image, or up to nine reference images. Keep Happy Horse 1.0 for legacy video-edit jobs.

media.caption: Happy Horse 1.1 example
media.description: Native-audio text, image and reference video route
media.altContext: Happy Horse 1.1 native audio reference-guided video example

features.title: Native audio
features.body: Generate dialogue, ambience and SFX with the render when the route supports it.

features.title: Text or image
features.body: Start from a scene brief or a still image to lock subject and composition.

features.title: Reference images
features.body: Use up to nine references with character1 through character9 prompt anchors.

features.title: Expanded ratios
features.body: Use landscape, vertical, square, classic, wide, tall, 5:4 or 4:5 composition.

features.title: 720p or 1080p
features.body: Choose the exposed MaxVideoAI resolution before generation.

features.title: Lower 1080p provider rate
features.body: Happy Horse 1.1 uses the current 1080p provider rate before MaxVideoAI margin.

decisionCards.title: Happy Horse 1.1 or Seedance?
decisionCards.body: Use Happy Horse 1.1 for Alibaba native-audio text, image and reference generation. Use Seedance 2.0 when multimodal references, longer production continuity and current Seedance behavior are the priority.

decisionCards.title: Need video edit?
decisionCards.body: Use Happy Horse 1.0 only when you specifically need the legacy video-edit endpoint. New text, image and reference jobs should start on 1.1.

decisionCards.title: Working from references?
decisionCards.body: Assign each file one job: identity, wardrobe, movement, environment or audio mood.

referenceWorkflows.title: Text-to-video
referenceWorkflows.body: Write the subject, action, camera, style and audio beats in a compact brief.

referenceWorkflows.title: Image-to-video
referenceWorkflows.body: Use a still image to anchor subject, product, wardrobe or composition.

referenceWorkflows.title: Reference-to-video
referenceWorkflows.body: Name each reference as character1, character2 and onward to keep roles clear.

referenceWorkflows.title: Legacy video edit
referenceWorkflows.body: Switch to Happy Horse 1.0 only when a source video must be edited rather than regenerated.

referenceWorkflows.title: Audio handling
referenceWorkflows.body: Keep dialogue short and tie SFX to visible actions for cleaner synchronized output.

pricingCopy.title: Happy Horse 1.1 pricing at a glance

pricingCopy.footnote: All prices are MaxVideoAI display prices in USD credits for preset scenarios.

demo.title: Demo prompt - Happy Horse 1.1
demo.prompt: Four-shot energetic studio food-film sequence in a small night market noodle stall after rain. Shot 1: neon reflections on wet pavement, a chef silhouette places a black wok over a blue gas flame, steam already rising. Shot 2: macro close-up of noodles flipping in the wok with orange sparks, camera locked, sizzling oil and quick whoosh. Shot 3: medium side shot as the chef slides the noodles into a ceramic bowl, steam curls across the lens, background lanterns soft and out of focus, no signs or readable text. Shot 4: slow push-in on the finished bowl on a stainless counter while rain taps the awning and steam fades into the neon light, no dialogue, no logos.

summary.subject: Night market noodle stall chef
summary.action: Flips noodles in a wok and plates the bowl after rain
summary.camera: Neon wide shot, macro wok, side plate-up, slow push-in
summary.style: Cinematic food film, wet street reflections, steam and lantern bokeh
summary.output: Wok sizzle, oil whoosh, rain on the awning, no dialogue
